title: 10 precautions to take when using Ocimum tenuiflorum (Holy Basil) for medicinal purposes
intro: Ocimum tenuiflorum, also known as holy basil, is a versatile herb with numerous medicinal properties, but it's crucial to take certain precautions while using it. First, pregnant and breastfeeding women should avoid using it as it may have adverse effects on the fetus or infant. Second, people with low blood sugar levels should use it with caution as it may lower blood sugar levels further. Third, holy basil may interact with certain medications like blood thinners, sedatives, and antidepressants, so consult your healthcare provider before use. Fourth, it's important to use it in moderation, as excessive consumption may lead to stomach upset, nausea, or dizziness. Lastly, always opt for high-quality, organic holy basil to ensure safety and efficacy, and follow recommended dosages to avoid potential side effects.
